Due to the introductionof Tram-Train vehicles it is neccessary to in stall a raised check rail to S&C units on the planned route. The raised checks are subject to a deviation to Group Standard GCRT5212 as they encroach into the lower sector structure gauge ref 15-108-DEV, which was authorised on 18-12-2015. The raised checks will be 50 ARL new but can be a maximum of 60mm ARL in their maximum worn condition. Compatibility with Trollies / RRV"s & OTP should be checked before traversing or working through the S&C. It shold be noted that the raised check rails do not  encroach into the standard lower sector vehicle gauges. Also see Product Acceptance Certificate PA/05/06394 for more details. The planned date of installation of raised check rail at this location is 28-05-2016.

Local Name: New Permanent Railway Infrastructure - Car Stop Markers at Magna Tram Stop
Free Text: As part of the Magna Tram Train project, 2 new (yellow) car stop marker posts have been installed on the site beyond each platform. These markers are a permanent asset and form part of the operational layout for the location. These are in place to enable the accurate stopping position of rolling stock. They must not be removed, relocated or altered during maintenance activities. Any work planned in the vicinity of these markers must take account of their position.

Local Name: Red Zone Working Prohibited - Magna Tram Train Station
Free Text: As part of the new Magna Tram Train Station, red zone working is prohibited. All work activities must be planned and conducted making use of safe systems of work.

Local Name: New Permanent Railway Infrastructure - 6ft Fencing - Magna Tram Train Station
Free Text: As part of the new Magna Tram Train Station, a section of new 6ft fencing alongside the tracks. This fencing is a permanent safety installation intended to restrict unauthorised access and support safe operational boundaries. It must not be removed/relocated/altered during any maintenance activities and it must be protected during any works in the immediate vicinity. Spares for the fencing have been procured by the project.

Local Name: No tamping through Magna Tram Train Station
Free Text:
Tamping is prohibited through the new Magna tram stop without prior approval by TME to maintain compliant track position.

The permanent way through Magna Station is constructed with specialised track infrastructure designed to maintain precise alignments, Lateral End Restraint Plates (LERPs) and coffin blocks have been installed: this section of track is subject to tight geometric and clearance tolerances as there is a requirement for level boarding between tram and platform. Minor changes to track geometry can result in non-compliant clearances or interface risks with surrounding assets. The track fixity is enhanced through platforms to control movement and resist settlement, this measure has been taken for the safe operation of the station environment and is not compatible with conventional tamping procedures.

This applies between 3m19ch - 3m543ch marked by "Warning Do Not Tamp" signage attached to the sleepers in the 4ft.

ELR Name: TOTLEY TUNNEL EAST - CHINLEY NTH JN (DORE & CHINLEY LINE)(D16)
Free Text:
The access point is located adjacent to the Bamford East REB. Access to the track is via a set of steps. ~
Under the TWA Network Rail [Hope Valley Capacity] Order maintenance access is permitted along the private track that leads to Thorpe Farm from the A6187 Castleton Road. The track passes beneath U/B MAS23 which has a height restriction of 3.5m.~
There is no vehicle parking immediately next to the access gate. Do not park on the single lane track adjacent to the gate as this will block the farm access. Parking is available for two light vehicles on the left hand side of the track leading up to the bridge - DO NOT restrict the width of the access.~
Care must be taken in manouvering vehicles beyond the bridge due the the presence of a drainage ditch. WHAT 3 WORDS RETRACT.HEARTLESS.NORTHWARD

ELR Name: TOTLEY TUNNEL EAST - CHINLEY NTH JN (DORE & CHINLEY LINE)(D16)
Free Text:
The pipeline was installed in 1954/55. It is 12" diameter and runs at 60 bar pressure. This very high, it"s about 30 times the air pressure in a car tyre. It transports mixed fuel.~
The pipe runs diagonally through the bridge, south west to north east.~
It has between 0.7m and 1.0m of cover.~
Beneath the road is has a reinforced concrete protection slab.~
There is a 3m exclusion zone around the pipeline.~
Inspections by helicoptor are undertaken every fortnight.The pipeline is owned and operated by Exolum Group <previously known as CLH PIPELINE SYSTEM <CLH-PS> LIMITED>.  ~
2nd Floor, 69 Wilson Street, London EC2A 2BB~
Recorded contacts in 2021, Pipeline Control Centre <Aldermaston> 01189 712 021, Pipeline Supervisor Mark Henesy 07540 300 959 and Pipeline Technician Callum Blades, 07821 635986.~
In emergency first call 999, then contact Exolum. Refer to CLH-PS Incidents and Emergencies.
